$comment: Canonical Language cell schema. Every kernel (Python, Go, Rust, TS) reads this template and interns Language cells against it. Per-language definitions (python.lang.json, typescript.lang.json, etc.) populate the shape and are written separately. See docs/coherence-substrate/language-cells.md for the architecture.

grammar_rule_kind.$comment: Grammar rule node kinds. inst = kind code, children carry parameters. Two rules with identical kind + children intern to the same NodeID.

emit_rule_kind.$comment: Emission template node kinds. inst = 0x80 | kind to keep namespace disjoint from grammar rules under the same RBasic slot.

token_classes.$comment: Built-in token-class names. New token classes are kernel writes (a per-name matcher), but most languages cover their needs with these four. Rare lexer needs (heredocs, regex literals) get language-specific token classes added on demand.

$language_cell_inst_comment: Reserved inst marker for the Language cell itself (vs grammar/emit rules under the same RBasic slot). 0xFF stays disjoint from kind codes (≤9) and emit-flagged codes (0x80..0x85).
$per_language_definition_shape: A per-language file (e.g. python.lang.json) carries: name, version, grammar (a tree of {kind, children} matching grammar_rule_kind), emission (matching emit_rule_kind), stdlib (list of {name, recipe-id}), numeric_defaults (list of {name, format-name from numeric-formats.canonical.json}).

per_language_template.$comment: Skeleton for a per-language definition. Populate grammar and emission; reference stdlib by recipe-id (computed elsewhere) and numeric_defaults by format-name (matches numeric-formats.canonical.json).
per_language_template.name: <language-name>

grammar.$comment: Root grammar rule. Recursively shaped as {kind: <one of grammar_rule_kind>, children: [...]}.

named_rules.$comment: Optional table of name → rule. RULE_REF nodes in `grammar` resolve through this table at registration time.

stdlib.$comment: Each entry binds a surface name to a substrate recipe. The recipe-id refers to a cell that exists prior to language registration (e.g. interned by the kernel's stdlib bootstrap).

numeric_defaults.$comment: Per-language numeric type name → format name (from numeric-formats.canonical.json).

$cross_language_identity_contract: A captured recipe under ctor name X with the same inner tree shape interns to the same NodeID regardless of which Language cell produced it. This is the load-bearing invariant: ctor naming and child ordering are the cross-language convergence point. Two languages disagree on syntax but MUST agree on the post-CAPTURE recipe shape — that is the substrate's claim to language-blindness.
